top_signals.title: Mercuria shipping head says fuel shortages could idle 10% of global fleet
top_signals.summary: The shipping sector is fast approaching a fuel crisis that could paralyze a tenth of the global fleet, Larry Johnson, global head of freight at commodities trading house Mercuria, said in an interview. Since the Middle East war erupted, markets have been preoccupied with potential shortfalls in the diesel and jet fuel traditionally exported in ...

top_signals.title: Strait of Hormuz shutdown: A visual guide to world’s most critical oil chokepoint
top_signals.summary: Before the closure of the Strait of Hormuz – which has been closed to commercial shipping traffic since Feb. 28 – about 25% of the world’s seaborne oil trade and 20% of global LNG passed through the 21-nautical-mile passage between Iran and Oman. top_signals.title: LNG Shipping Rates Subdued Last Week
top_signals.summary: LNG The LNG market saw a relatively subdued week overall due to market participants being in Posidonia. Activity appeared steady, with values largely holding within narrow ranges before softening slightly towards the end of the week. On BLNG1 Australia–Japan rates edged higher over the course of the week, gaining $600 to settle at $78,400/day. The ...

top_signals.title: Intra-Asia Container Index Up 10% Last Week
top_signals.summary: The Drewry Intra-Asia Container Index (IACI), the benchmark widely referenced by procurement teams, increased 10% to $1,114 per 40ft container, a record high, after strengthening in 11 of the last 12 weeks. The index has now more than doubled in value since the US/Israeli attack on Iran. See detailed commentary below Freight rates surged; IACI ...

top_signals.title: Financing the EU aviation and shipping transition: From policy ambition to bankable pathways
top_signals.summary: Aviation and shipping are central to the EU’s economy and enable many other sectors to function, yet they remain almost entirely dependent on fossil fuels—with corresponding implications for other sectors’ supply chain (Scope 3) emissions. Decarbonizing these subsectors is essential to achieving the EU’s climate neutrality objective by 2050 and reducing transport emissions by 90% ...
